name: Current Affairs in History & Archaeology (2): 2025 Discoveries

sourceRefs.label: indiabeyondcurry.com/insightsonindia.com/arkeonews.net - 2025 archaeological discoveries (TN iron objects, Thar Harappan find, Kerala megaliths, Maharashtra labyrinth)

researchNotes: Second History/Heritage current-affairs topic (2nd lap). The lead fact — iron objects found at 6 Tamil Nadu sites, dated 5,000-5,400 years old (February 2025) — is especially significant for this TN-focused exam and politically resonant in the same way as the Keezhadi findings already covered in a6-tamil-society-archaeological-discoveries (Day 48), since it pushes back the dating of iron technology/civilization in the region. Expansion pass (Batch 13): added the Lankamala (Andhra Pradesh) inscription find, the independent-invention-vs-diffusion debate the TN iron find has reopened, and the ASI's 3D-scanning/photogrammetry digitisation push — this was the thinnest topic in the batch.

reading.intro: Covers major 2025 Indian archaeological discoveries, with particular attention to the Tamil Nadu iron-objects find — directly extending the already-shipped Day-48 topic on Arikkamedu/Keezhadi with a newer, equally significant discovery.

qa.question: What significant archaeological discovery was made in Tamil Nadu in February 2025?
qa.answer: Iron objects were discovered at SIX sites across Tamil Nadu, all believed to be between 5,000 and 5,400 years old — a highly significant finding for understanding the early use of iron technology in the region.

qa.question: What first-of-its-kind Harappan discovery was made in 2025, and where?
qa.answer: Harappan civilization remains (pottery, bangles, bricks, a furnace, ancient wall remains) were found in the Thar Desert, near Jaisalmer (close to the Pakistan border) — the FIRST time artifacts from the 4,500-year-old Harappan civilization have been found in the Thar Desert specifically.

qa.question: What megalithic discovery did the ASI make in Kerala in March 2025?
qa.answer: Over 110 megaliths spread across 45 hectares of land in Palakkad, Kerala — expected to shed light on the early Iron Age society in Kerala.

qa.question: What is significant about the ancient structure discovered in Solapur district, Maharashtra?
qa.answer: Archaeologists uncovered India's LARGEST known ancient circular labyrinth, in the Boramani grasslands — approximately 50 by 50 feet, made of 15 concentric stone circles, dated to nearly 2,000 years ago and linked to the Satavahana dynasty.

qa.question: What recent expansion has the ASI made to its archaeological research capabilities?
qa.answer: The ASI expanded its Underwater Archaeology Wing in 2025, focusing on exploring coastal waters in Maharashtra, Gujarat, and Tamil Nadu to locate submerged historical sites.

qa.question: What ancient inscriptions did the ASI discover at Kadapa's Lankamala Reserve Forest in Andhra Pradesh, and what do they suggest?
qa.answer: Ancient inscriptions believed to date from the 4th to 16th century CE — suggesting that Lankamala was a major Shaivite pilgrimage centre, regularly visited by devotees even from as far as North India.

qa.question: What is significant about the timing/method of the Tamil Nadu iron-objects discovery in relation to the broader 'Out of India'/independent-invention debate?
qa.answer: The 5,000-5,400-year-old dating (older than comparable global iron-working evidence in some cases) has led scientists to question whether people in India extracted, smelted, and shaped iron tools independently, rather than the technology diffusing in from elsewhere — an active, unresolved research question rather than a settled fact.

qa.question: What kind of research techniques is the ASI increasingly applying to document its archaeological sites and finds?
qa.answer: 3D scanning and photogrammetry, to create detailed digital/virtual records and models of major archaeological sites, monuments, and artefacts — part of a broader digitisation push alongside physical excavation work.

The ASI's Underwater Archaeology Wing, expanded in 2025, focuses on coastal waters in Maharashtra, Gujarat, and which other state?
Ancient inscriptions discovered at Kadapa's Lankamala Reserve Forest (Andhra Pradesh) suggest the site was a major centre for which tradition?
The Lankamala inscriptions are dated to approximately which period?
What debate has the early dating of Tamil Nadu's 2025 iron-object discovery reopened?
options: Whether India had a Bronze Age
Whether iron technology in India was independently developed or diffused from elsewhere
Whether TN had contact with Rome
questions.explanation: Independent development vs diffusion of iron technology.
